mnemonics: White Space Is Like Java's Breathing Room - It Makes Code Readable And Organized
keyPoints: Whitespace (spaces, tabs, newlines) is ignored by the Java compiler
It is used for formatting code to improve human readability
It separates keywords, identifiers, and operators
commonMistakes: Inconsistent indentation making code hard to follow
Using tabs and spaces interchangeably leading to misaligned code in different editors
Believing whitespace affects program logic or performance
